pattern: Spelling a number splits it into three-digit groups scaled by thousand, million and billion; each group is rendered with hundreds, tens and units helpers and the non-empty group words are joined with their scale word.
hints: Break the number into chunks of three digits from the least significant end; each chunk gets a scale word (thousand, million, billion) except the lowest.
Write a helper that turns any value below one thousand into words, handling the hundreds digit, the irregular numbers below twenty, and the tens words.
Render each non-zero three-digit group with that helper, append its scale word, join the pieces with single spaces, and special-case zero as the word Zero.

edge_inputs.description: three-digit number with hundreds and tens
five-digit number crossing the thousand group
seven-digit number into the million group
zero is spelled out as a single word
exact hundred with no tens or units
embedded zero group must not emit a stray scale word
value with a zero middle group
maximum 32-bit value spanning the billion group

stress.description: random values across the whole range to exercise every group, scale word and zero-group placement
